summary: Two-story rectangular brick building with a dark stepped gable roof, continuous horizontal trim bands and regularly spaced windows on both floors. The structure sits on a raised white base/ledge and has stone/stonebrick corner pillars; wooden plank horizontal band separates the floors. Roof is dark (netherbrick) with light stone slab accents along eaves.

shape.overall_form: simple rectangular box with a symmetrical peaked/stepped roof along the long axis
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetrical along the center ridge (front/back mirror)

elements: rows of small rectangular windows evenly spaced on both floors (glass panes)
raised white stone/quartz border/base around building footprint
horizontal wooden plank band between floors
dark netherbrick stepped/gabled roof with light slab accents along eaves
rebuild_hints: Footprint is a simple rectangle with a one-block-wide white/quartz border around it; build main walls inset from that border by one block.
Make two full-height storeys of brick walls with a continuous wooden plank band between them and a quartz strip above and below the band matching images.
Place small 1x1 (or 1x2) glass windows in a regular repeating pattern across front and sides (about 6 across the long face per floor in these views).
Use stonebrick (or similar) vertical corner pillars that are 1-2 blocks tall interrupting the horizontal bands at corners.
Construct a symmetrical gabled roof using netherbrick stairs/blocks with stepped profile and place light-colored slabs (quartz or smooth stone) along the eaves and at the gable ends.
uncertainties: Exact interior layout, ceiling heights, and floor thickness are not visible.
Material identification between quartz/smoothstone/polished variants for the white trim is uncertain.
Corner pillar material might be stonebrick or andesite/stone variants; texture looks like stonebrick but not certain.
Precise block counts and the building grid alignment are estimated from images; actual width/depth/height may differ by a few blocks.
